RT @shao__meng: 最近顾问的团队招聘，面试过程中对两家公司印象很深：小冰和 Pika。

The background noise in open offices, in multiple experiments, decreases the ability to do analytical work, increase bugs in software, and decrease the ability to find bugs.

Open floor plans are just a bad idea for any solo work. https://t.co/Y2KX5HjIEd

Noise cancelling headphones are apparently a futile solution to office noise… they mostly delude us into thinking they help. This study finds we feel they improve our ability to concentrate, but found no significant improvements in actual concentration. https://t.co/KrX8JIQxuO https://t.co/EoUNyVn9cr

BREAKING: Perplexity Computer just made finance analysts, consultants, and research teams look like an expensive line item.

Here are the exact prompts replacing them:

DM me "Research" to get the full playbook free. https://t.co/HIzXCyZsoL

1/ Finance: The SEC Research Analyst

Prompt:
"You are a senior equity research analyst.
Pull the latest 10-K and 10-Q filings for [COMPANY].

Extract revenue trends, risk disclosures, management commentary, and year-over-year margin changes.

Cross-reference current analyst

2/ Solopreneurs: The Competitor Intelligence Agent

Prompt:
"Monitor [COMPETITOR HANDLES] on X for the past 7 days. Track engagement patterns, hook formats, bookmark-to-like ratios, and content gaps.

Identify which topics drive the most saves. Compile a full gap analysis showing

3/ Consultants: The Strategy Deck Builder

Prompt:
"You are a senior strategy consultant. Conduct a full competitive analysis of [INDUSTRY/COMPANY].

Cover market positioning, Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, pricing strategy, and customer targeting. Identify 3 white space

Inside the Perplexity Mastery Guide:
→ 10 strategic use cases with pro tips
→ 5 Deep Research prompt templates
→ A Meta-Prompt that builds custom workflows for any goal

(Must be following @godofprompt)
